QF.id: VB Fund Distribution and Channel Management_26

question.text: Under the SEBI (Prohibition of Fraudulent and Unfair Trade Practices) Regulations 2003, mis-selling in mutual funds includes all of the following EXCEPT __________.

answer.text: Recommending a scheme after conducting a proper risk assessment and suitability analysis of the investor
Making false or misleading statements to induce the purchase or sale of mutual fund units
Concealing or omitting material facts about the scheme from the investor
Not taking reasonable care to ensure suitability of the scheme for the investor

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Per SEBI PFUTP 2003, mis-selling includes four activities: (1) Making false/misleading statements, (2) Concealing/omitting material facts, (3) Concealing associated risk factors, and (4) Not taking reasonable care to ensure suitability. Recommending a scheme after proper risk assessment and suitability analysis is the correct practice and is NOT mis-selling.
**The Trap:** All three distractors are actual components of the mis-selling definition. The question tests whether you can distinguish proper advisory conduct from the four prohibited mis-selling activities defined by SEBI.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Mis-selling is a critical topic in NISM V-B and V-A. Distributors must understand all four elements to avoid regulatory action.
**Key Takeaway:** Mis-selling = false statements + concealing facts + concealing risks + not ensuring suitability. Proper suitability assessment is the antidote to mis-selling.
